  • Abdulbasit Gulsen’s blog post on cds-numberrange-plugin https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/a-cap-plugin-for-flexible-number-range-management-cds-numberrange-plugin/ba-p/14129453  
  • The cds-numberrange-plugin repo on GitHub https://github.com/abdulbasitg/cds-numberrange-plugin 
  • The cds-numberrange-plugin entry on Best of cap-js https://bestofcapjs.org/#/packages/cds-numberrange-plugin  

 

SAPUI5 version preview in SAP Build Work Zone 

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-sapui5-version-preview-in-sap-build-work-zone/ba-p/14117996 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 SEA CodeJam roadshow promotion 

0:58 SAP Open Source Webinar 

1:58 SAP community contributions 

3:58 SAPUI5 version preview in SAP Build Work Zone 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for June the 19th, 2025.  

[M K Sheena] Hello, everyone. We have an exciting news for the SAP developer community in Southeast Asia. The SAP Code Sam Roadshow is coming your way this July with stops in Singapore, Malaysia, and Thailand from July 2nd to July 9th. Shilfa, Srini, and I are gearing up to deliver full-day hands-on sessions on your favorite BTP topics. Here is the agenda. July 2nd and July 3rd, we'll be in Singapore and we'll cover ABAP Cloud and SAP Gen AI. July 7th, we'll meet in Kuala Lumpur to focus on SAP Build. And finally, on July 9th, we'll be in Bangkok to explore SAP Gen AI again. The registrations will be closed shortly, so don't miss this chance to participate in this exciting event. Stay tuned and get ready to learn with us.  

[Mamikee] Mark your calendars, on July 9th, SAP is hosting a special open source webinar to kick off your journey with the brand new open source SAP Architecture Center. Built on GitHub and design for architects and developers alike, the Architecture Center is your hub for solution reference diagrams, component lists, and real-world SAP BTP scenarios, all aimed at helping you design efficient, scalable enterprise landscapes. You'll hear from SAP product managers who will walk you through how this platform evolves from last year's BTP solution diagrams to today's collaborative architectural ecosystem. system now fully open source now whether you are building clean core extensions or mapping out full stack SAP solutions this is your chance to connect learn and contribute you can learn more about this event on in the description below. 

[DJ] wanted to just highlight a couple of recent SAP community contributions first there's a great blog post from Mauricio Lauffer all about BTP runtimes it's a very readable post written by a human being with opinions opinions that are clearly one from experience and maybe a few battle scars as well we learn from each other and especially when opinions are expressed and explained and when Mauricio expresses opinions i listen he covers runtimes languages performance scalability developer experience devops and lots more and ends with his own top picks which are in his words direct and unapologetic that is what i like anyway head over to the blog post and have a read over coffee even better let Mauricio know what your thoughts are in a comment we also have a new CAP plugin contribution by Abdul Basit ghulzan and it's called cds number range plugin and as the name suggests it allows you to easily add ranges to your cap apps and services if you're familiar with number ranges in the app stack then this will be very familiar to you as well basically you can define your own number ranges and then use annotations to define the fields to decorate the fields the elements that you want these number ranges to be valid for anyway as Mike Zashka said in a comment this plugin elevates number ranges to first class citizens that's a really nice way of thinking about it anyway head over to Abdelbasid's blog post, his GitHub repo, and also thanks to Marian Seiss, Best of JS, where it's also featured. See you online.  

[Nico] Hi, everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. There's an exciting new feature in SAP Build Work Zone that lets you preview the latest UI5 version before it hits your productive system. You can now duplicate your site, enable the latest UI5 version for that site, and then test it, check it out, make sure it works as expected before you actually use it in production. This new feature is in response to customer feedback, so thank you very much. It's always very much appreciated. Thank you. Now, go check out that new feature, and I hope to talk to you soon. Bye.

entry.title: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The insights shared in this blog are based on my personal observations and learning. They may not apply universally, and I encourage readers to conduct their own research and make an informed decision before using this content for productive use.
 
Introduction
As organizations increasingly adopt low-code/no-code platforms like SAP Build Process Automation, the ability for developers to independently create and deploy automation projects across systems has brought both speed and efficiency to operations. With minimal technical barriers, business users and developers alike can prototype and launch automations rapidly—streamlining approvals, triggering backend processes, and replacing manual tasks.
However, this empowerment introduces a new set of challenges, particularly in managing what happens after a project is built. In traditional software delivery, deployments to critical systems—like production—follow tightly controlled paths with approvals, validations, and audits. But in environments with rapid automation, these controls are often missing or too lightweight.
 
Business Challenge:
In SAP Build Process Automation, users with the Developer role have the ability to:
• Create multiple automation projects.
• Transport projects between environments, including into production.
While this flexibility is essential for innovation, it raises a serious governance concern: Who is monitoring these transports? Who ensures that only validated, reviewed projects reach production? Without safeguards, even an accidental deployment can cause business process disruptions, data inconsistencies, or security loopholes.
Earlier, there was no built-in mechanism to regulate or oversee such activities. As a result, organizations relied heavily on manual communication, post-facto monitoring, or restrictive access controls—which either slowed down innovation or left gaps in accountability.
 
The Turning Point: Governance Rules
To address this gap, Governance Rules were introduced. This feature allows administrators to define structured approval processes and enforce checks before a project is transported between systems—especially into sensitive environments like production.
With Governance Rules, you can:
• Create mandatory approval workflows before project transport.
• Specify who can approve transports based on project type, environment, or organizational policy.
• Track and audit all transport requests, decisions, and outcomes.
• Enable role-based control without completely blocking development agility.
This creates a safety net that ensures only reviewed and authorized changes make it into business-critical systems.
 
Business Impact:
The introduction of Governance Rules has shifted the paradigm from reactive issue management to proactive risk prevention. Organizations benefit from:  
• Improved compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
• Reduced risk of unintentional disruptions in production.
• Clear audit trails for all project movements and approvals.
• Increased confidence in scaling automation across departments.
Moreover, it encourages a culture of shared responsibility—developers retain the ability to build freely, while governance teams ensure stability and alignment with enterprise standards.
 
Prerequisites
You’ve created a service instance and service key for SAP Build Process Automation. Note down the service key credentials for configuring the destination.
You've the Process Automation Admin role, which allows you to perform these steps.
You've set up the destination and added the following properties in your SAP BTP subaccount.

Destination Configuration
FieldValue
NameAs per your choice. For example, sap_process_automation_service_user_token_exchange.
TypeHTTP
URL<”endpoints”.”api”>
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2UserTokenExchange
Client ID<"uaa"."clientid”>
Client Secret<"uaa"."clientsecret”>
Token Service URL<"uaa"."url">/oauth/token
Additional Properties 
FieldValue
sap.processautomation.enabledTRUE
 
 
Ensure you've created the environment and allow-list the destination in the Governance environment by navigating to Control Tower > Destinations > Add. Search and select the destination, click Next, select 'Specific Environment', select 'Governance', and then click
'Add Destination'.
 
 
 
 
 
Now, click on the Governance Rule tile under Control Tower.
 
 
Now, you can choose the scenario for which you want to activate the Governance Rule. You have the option to set it up for both Create Project and Transport Project. By default, the setting is Ungoverned. To switch to Governed, you’ll need to create the project by clicking the Create button.
 

 

 

A pre-configured project will be created automatically. You can customize it by adding additional validations or modifying parameters such as the approver or you can directly deploy it to the Governance Environment.
 
 
 
While deploying, select the destination(sap_process_automation_service_user_token_exchange) that was created and added under your Build Control Tower.

 

 

 
Now, if you go back and check under Governance Rules, you'll see that the Governed option is enabled. Select it and click Confirm.
 

 

 
The setup for Transport Project Governance is now complete. From now on, whenever someone attempts to transport a project from the Development system to the Production system, the defined governance rules will be enforced.
 

 

 

 
After clicking on Transport, a questionnaire form will appear, which the submitter needs to fill out and submit for review.
 
 

 

 

 

 

Once the request is submitted, admins can view all requests along with their current statuses. Only the designated approver has the authority to approve or reject the request.
 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion:
Governance Rules are not just a feature—they represent a strategic enabler for controlled automation growth. By embedding checks, balances, and transparency into the deployment lifecycle, businesses can confidently expand their automation footprint without compromising on control or compliance. In fast-paced environments where change is constant, Governance Rules serve as a foundation for sustainable, secure, and scalable innovation.

entry.title: Driving Clean Core Adoption with SAP BTP: Highlights
entry.content:
Why Clean Core—and Why Now?

“Clean Core” is not another buzzword. It’s about making your SAP landscape future-proof by reducing technological "debt" of custom code and shifting to scalable, upgrade-safe extensions via SAP BTP.

 

Why it matters?

  • Quicker innovation cycles
  • Lower TCO over time
  • Simplified upgrades and landscape evolution
  • Readiness for AI, mobile, and cloud-native use cases

 

Agenda highlights & session recap

Welcome & Introduction - Speaker: Luis Felipe Lanz (SAP)

  • Set the stage with SAP’s strategic push on Clean Core
  • Call to action: Start with a use case, not a product

 [Link to presentation]

 

The Road to Clean Core: Why is it a Necessity? - Speaker: César Martín (SAP)

  • In-app, side-by-side, and developer extensibility options
  • SAP’s evolution from modification-heavy models
  • SAP BTP as the enabler of sustainable extensibility

[Link to presentation]

 

SAP Cloud ALM Supporting Clean Core - Speaker: Prasanna Vedhartham (SAP)

  • Overview of Cloud ALM capabilities
  • Lifecycle orchestration as a driver of governance
  • Role of ALM in keeping extensions compliant and traceable

[Link to presentation]

 

SAP BTP AI Core and AI Capabilities - Speaker: Fernando Terry (SAP)

  • BTP’s foundation for AI consumption and integration
  • Clean Core as prerequisite for scalable AI use
  • Showcased AI scenarios already in production

[Link to presentation]

 

ABAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model) - Speaker: Daniel Álamo (SAP)

  • RAP for clean, upgrade-safe ABAP extensions
  • Strong focus on side-by-side clean development principles

[Link to presentation]

 

Success Story: A2K2C - Speaker: David Yáñez (SAP)

  • Journey from custom-heavy legacy to modular BTP setup
  • Using AI to help building the foundation of a Clean Core

[Link to presentation]

 

Success Story: Transformation lessons - Speakers: Maria Fernandez & Elton Machado (NTT DATA)

  • Clean Core implementation at scale across industries
  • Emphasis on managing change across large teams
  • BTP extensions in action and tools to analyze your custom code

[Link to presentation]

 

Acceleration Packs & SAP Mobile - Speakers: Humberto Ordosgoitia & Ece Dagasan (SAP)

  • Overview of Mobile Start and prebuilt accelerators
  • Quick wins and live demo

[Link to presentation]

 

Hands-On Workshop (Creating apps with Joule) - Speaker: Daniel Álamo (SAP)

  • Live app creation using Joule
  • Demonstrated how BTP tools simplify clean extensions

 

Closing Notes / Adoption and Next Steps - Speaker: Oswaldo Seara (SAP)

  • How to engage with your BTP CSM and move forward
  • SAP initiatives to support the Clean Core and how can we help you to achieve it

[Link to presentation]

 

Key Takeaways

  • Clean Core is a strategic advantage, not a restriction

  • SAP BTP is the execution layer for extensibility, AI, and integration
  • Workshops show it’s not just theory—you can build now

 

What’s Next?

Here’s how to keep the momentum going:

  • 💬Contact your SAP BTP Customer Success Manager
  • 🎓Visit SAP Discovery Center Missions on BTP

entry.title: Application Frontend Service now generally available in SAP BTP Trail Account [cf us10-XXX]
entry.content:
As part of our exciting  SAP Build release, we're happy to announce the release of Application Frontend service, a new SaaS application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Trail account for US region for hosting, serving and operating frontend applications.

SAP BTP Trial accounts can be deployed with limited to five(5) applications per subaccount.

Now, you can enjoy the following benefits of Application Frontend Service

What is Application Frontend Service?

Host, serve, and manage frontend applications to extend SAP business solutions.

Application Frontend service lets you host and serve frontend applications. It serves as a single entry point for hosting web-based UI applications to extend SAP's business solutions. The service provides tools for deploying and managing applications, serving static resources, routing backend calls to defined targets, and observability tools.

Application Frontend service is included in the SAP Build.

Application Frontend service supports various channels for managing applications, such as the commonly used Generic Application Content Deployer (GACD) for multitarget applications (MTA), a dedicated UI in the cockpit, and a CLI. These options allow you to choose the channel that best suits your needs.

Application Frontend is a SaaS application which is not coupled to any specific SAP BTP runtime environment or other SAP BTP applications. You can find and subscribe to the service from the SAP BTP cockpit. Application Frontend is also equipped with a service broker to support interaction flows using service instances, for example when using MTA deploy in the CF environment. 

While there is native support in the good old MTA concept for lifecycle management, Application Frontend also provides a command line interface (CLI) as a first-class tool for managing your applications. With the CLI, you can directly interact with the service and execute all actions supported by Application Frontend to enjoy fast response times and new integration opportunities.

High Level Overview of Application Frontend Service - Deployment

Prerequisites to enable Application Frontend Serivce

  • Go to your SAP BTP Trail Account - Subaccount
  • Go to Entitlements - Select Application Frontend Service
  • Select 2 checkbox for enabling the Plan (Developer and Application) 

Go to Instances and Subscription - Select Application Frontend Service from dropdown with Plan - Trial

Go to Instances and Subscription - Select Application Frontend Service with Plan - Developer

Create a New Role Collection

Assign Role to newly created Role Collection

Application Frontend Service Library & CLI (Command Line)

npm install -g /appfront-cli

afctl login --sso -a <api_endpoint>

afctl push

 

Conclusion

Application Frontend Service is a game-changer for Cloud Foundry users. By simplifying UI development, enhancing scalability, and promoting consistency, AFS empowers developers to create high-quality applications more efficiently. As cloud-native development continues to evolve, integrating tools like AFS will be crucial in staying ahead of the curve and delivering exceptional user experiences.

For more detailed insights, you can check out the original blog posts : 

  • Introducing Application Frontend Service : Application Frontend Service 
  • Simple UI Applications with Application Frontend Service : DEV Community
  • Application Frontend Service Help Documentation : Guide and Development

entry.title: Integrating Microsoft Services with SAP Build Process Automation using Microsoft 365 SDK & Graph API
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The insights shared in this blog are based on my personal observations and learning. They may not apply universally, and I encourage readers to conduct their own research and make an informed decision before using this content for productive use.

 

Introduction

In the modern digital workplace, automation is no longer a luxury—it's a necessity. Businesses are continuously looking for ways to connect systems, eliminate manual effort, and accelerate decision-making. SAP Build Process Automation offers a powerful platform for automating business workflows, while Microsoft 365 services—like Outlook, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Excel—are deeply embedded in everyday enterprise operations.

This blog explores how to securely integrate SAP Build Process Automation with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), enabling direct access to Microsoft 365 services through either the Microsoft 365 SDK or Microsoft Graph API. This integration opens up automation possibilities across a wide range of business functions—from reading and sending emails, to accessing SharePoint documents, managing Excel files, and interacting with OneDrive storage.

Business Challenge: Automating Cross-Platform Enterprise Processes

Enterprise workflows often span multiple systems and platforms—SAP for business logic, and Microsoft 365 for communication, document management, and collaboration. However, bridging these platforms typically requires manual data transfers, file handling, and task coordination. These repetitive, error-prone activities consume valuable time and create bottlenecks in business processes.

Key challenges include:

  • Limited out-of-the-box automation SDK for Microsoft services.
  • Security and compliance risks due to manual authentication and permission handling.
  • Lack of centralized access to documents, emails, spreadsheets, and collaboration data from within automation tools.

With this integration, organizations can:

  • Automate email operations without outlook.
  • Interact with SharePoint and OneDrive to manage and retrieve files.
  • Automate Excel data processing and reporting.
  • Ensure secure and controlled access using Microsoft Entra ID authentication.

By embedding Microsoft 365 services directly into SAP Build Process Automation, businesses can streamline end-to-end workflows across systems, enhance productivity, and reduce IT overhead.

 

Azure App Registration in Microsoft Entra ID

Log in to the Azure Portal:

Navigate to https://portal.azure.com.

Open Microsoft Entra ID under Azure Services.

Image 1

From the left navigation pane, click on App registrations and then New registration.

Image 2

Image 3

Provide a Name for the app, choose the appropriate Supported account types, and set the Redirect URI type to Web.
Enter the following URI:
https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient.

Image 4

After registration:

Copy the Tenant ID and Client ID.

Generate a Client Secret by going to Certificates & Secrets.

These credentials are required for both Microsoft 365 SDK and Graph API integrations.

Image 5

Image 6

Method 1: Integrate Using Microsoft 365 SDK

Open SAP Build Process Automation Lobby and navigate to Control Tower > External Authentication.

Image 7

Click Create New Authentication, select Microsoft 365, and enter:

Tenant ID, Client ID, Client Secret

Choose the required Scopes based on your use case.

Image 8

Open the Desktop Agent on your local machine.

Image 9

Now Navigate to Settings → External Authentication → Select the Microsoft Authentication entry and Sign in using your user or technical account.

Image 10

Image 11

Image 12

If you're a Global Admin, you can check Consent on behalf of your organization during the first login prompt.

Note: Individual users can also provide consent for their own accounts.

Image 13

Set this authentication entry as your Default Connection in the Desktop Agent.

Image 14

In your Process Automation Project:

Add the Microsoft 365 Cloud SDK as a dependency.

Image 15

Use built-in activities such as:

Search Emails (e.g., filter by folder: Inbox or by subject)

Send Email (e.g., specify recipient, subject, and body)

Image 16

Upon execution, you can validate:

Emails fetched from the inbox

Outgoing emails successfully delivered to recipients

Image 17

Image 18

Image 19

Image 20

Image 21

Method 2: Integrate Using Microsoft Graph API

For more advanced or customized use cases, you can use the Microsoft Graph API to interact with Microsoft services programmatically.

Permissions Setup:

Review and assign required permissions from Microsoft Graph API Reference docs.

Use Graph Explorer to test API calls and review responses.

If permissions require admin approval, click Grant admin consent for Default Directory.

 

Image 22

Image 23

Image 24

Image 25

Image 26

Image 27

Image 28

Generate Access Token:

Use your Tenant ID, Client ID, and Client Secret to form the authentication request.

Refer to standard OAuth 2.0 token generation flow (as shown in Image 29).

Image 29

Image 30

Call the Graph API:

Use the obtained Access Token to make API calls to endpoints such as:
https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{UserID}/mailFolders('Inbox')/messages

Fetch email data or other resources as needed (refer to Image 32 for sample response).

Image 31

Image 32

Conclusion: Unified Automation Across SAP and Microsoft Ecosystems

Integrating SAP Build Process Automation with Microsoft Entra ID and Microsoft 365 services delivers a robust foundation for enterprise-grade automation. Whether using the Microsoft 365 SDK for a plug-and-play experience, or leveraging Microsoft Graph API for advanced customization, this approach allows seamless access to:

  • Outlook – for reading, searching, and sending emails
  • SharePoint & OneDrive – for retrieving, storing, and managing documents
  • Excel – for accessing and automating spreadsheet-based operations
  • Teams & Calendar (optional) – for integrating communication and scheduling meetings

Security is built-in through OAuth-based authentication and consent management, ensuring compliance while enabling automation at scale.

By centralizing access and automating repetitive actions across Microsoft and SAP platforms, businesses reduce complexity, minimize errors, and free up valuable resources. This is more than a technical integration—it's a strategic enabler for digital transformation.

Let’s shape the future together!

entry.title: Gamechanger Alert: Meet SAP Joule Studio for Real-World Impact
entry.content:
As someone who's still fairly new to SAP and learning about all it has to offer, I recently came across SAP Joule Studio—a tool that honestly surprised me. It’s part of SAP’s growing focus on AI, but instead of being just a tech buzzword, it’s something practical. Something helpful. Something that actually makes life easier.

So I thought I’d share what I’ve learned so far, for anyone else who’s curious like me.

 

What is SAP Joule Studio?

SAP Joule Studio is a low-code/no-code platform that lets you create AI-powered agents—little digital helpers that live inside your SAP system and support everyday tasks. These agents can:

  • Answer questions using real SAP data
  • Send reminders or alerts
  • Trigger workflows based on events
  • Help users navigate complex processes

The best part? You don’t need to be a developer to build one.

All you need is a good understanding of a business problem, and Joule Studio helps you turn that into a solution using easy visual tools.

 

Why This Is a Game-Changer (With Real Use Cases)

What really got me excited was seeing how real-world teams can use this tool to solve day-to-day challenges. Here are a few examples that really stood out to me:

✅HR & Employee Self-Service

  • Use Case: An AI agent that answers common HR questions like “How many vacation days do I have left?”, “What’s the process to update my bank details?”, or “When does my probation end?”
  • Impact: Reduces emails to HR, gives instant answers, and improves employee satisfaction.

✅Onboarding Assistant

  • Use Case: An AI agent that guides a new joiner through their first week—showing them how to complete paperwork, introducing them to teammates, and sending daily checklists.
  • Impact: Makes onboarding smoother, especially for remote hires or global teams.

✅Procurement Alerts

  • Use Case: A smart assistant that watches for delays from key suppliers and notifies procurement managers if a PO is stuck or overdue.
  • Impact: Keeps procurement responsive and improves relationships with vendors.

✅Project Management Summary

  • Use Case: A daily AI briefing that tells project leads about overdue tasks, timesheet gaps, or resource bottlenecks.
  • Impact: Saves hours of manual tracking and keeps teams aligned.

✅Compliance Checker

  • Use Case: A bot that checks which employees haven't completed their mandatory training or signed policy documents, and sends them a reminder.
  • Impact: Helps HR stay compliant without manual follow-ups.

✅IT Helpdesk Agent

  • Use Case: An agent that answers questions like “How do I reset my SAP password?” or “Where do I raise a ticket?”
  • Impact: Cuts down ticket volume for IT support and empowers users.

These aren’t just theoretical—they’re real issues we see in companies every day. And Joule Studio gives you a way to solve them quickly, without coding or waiting on IT.

 

Why Clients Will Love It Too

For clients, Joule Studio brings:

  • Self-service power to their SAP users
  • Less dependency on IT for small tasks
  • Smarter workflows with reminders, alerts, and guidance
  • Faster onboarding and training for new team members
  • Consistency across geographies and business units

AI becomes something helpful, not something scary or complex. It empowers users instead of overwhelming them.

 

Where You Can See It in Action

If you're like me and prefer learning visually, I highly recommend this official demo:

 SAP Joule Studio – AI for Everyone (YouTube)

You can also explore SAP’s official product page for more examples and how-to guides:
 SAP Joule Studio – Official Page

These are great starting points with visuals and real examples.

 

Getting Started with Joule Studio

If you’re ready to try it:

  1. Make sure your system is running on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform).
  2. Go into SAP Build and access Joule Studio.
  3. Start by creating a small AI agent for a common task.
  4. Train it using real process examples and test in Preview mode.
  5. Roll it out with the right user permissions.

 

 

 

Remember—you don’t need to be technical. You just need to understand your business and what people are struggling with. Joule Studio helps you close that gap.

 

Final Thoughts (From One Learner to Another)

I’m still exploring SAP, still learning, and still asking questions. But tools like Joule Studio give me hope, not just for where tech is going, but for how we can make it work for people.

This isn’t about replacing humans—it’s about supporting them with small, smart helpers that remove friction and free up time.

If you're also exploring SAP tools and wondering where to start with AI, this might just be the right place. Try building a simple use case and see what it unlocks for your team.

And if you’ve tried it, I’d really love to hear what you’ve built. Let’s share and learn together, because the best ideas often come from the people closest to the problem.

 

#SAPJoule, #JouleStudio, #SAPBTP, #AIinSAP, #Gamechanger, #NoCodeAI, #SAPBuild, #EnterpriseAI, #DigitalTransformation, #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Joule based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant - Transforming with Gen AI
entry.content:
In Early 2024, released first version of the SAP Business Application Studio extension which  Automates conversion of SAP HANA Database artifacts to SAP HANA Cloud artifacts.   SAP HANA  database objects (such as .hdbcds, .hdbtable, .hdbview, etc.) are transformed into their corresponding SAP CAP compliant database artifacts (e.g., .cds models).

The Q2-2025  release , enables service layer migration using GenAI capabilities in SAP Build Code. The assistant analyses SAP XS objects - xsodata ,xsjslib, xsjs and  files and generate corresponding service.cds, service.js and custom handler implementations under the CAP service layer. These AI capabilities  accelerates the migration of service layer while preserving structure and functional behaviour where possible. A sample conversion of xsodata to SAP CAP service definition is shown below. 

poWorklist.xsodata

service namespace "com.sap.openSAP.hana5.example.service"  { 
  "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_HEADER_EN" as "PO_WORKLIST" 
   keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_ITEM" as "PurchaseOrderItem" keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "PO.HeaderView" as "PurchaseOrderHeader" keys("PurchaseOrderId")
   navigates (     
        "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem" as "PurchaseOrderItems" from principal);

   association "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem"
      principal "PurchaseOrderHeader"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "1"
      dependent "PurchaseOrderItem"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "*";

}

service.cds

//converted from: /users/projects/hana-shine-xsa/core-xsjs/lib/xsodata/poWorklist.xsodata
service poWorklist @(path:'/poWorklist') {
    entity PO_WORKLIST as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_HEADER_EN {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PURCHASEORDERID
    };

    entity PurchaseOrderItem as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_ITEM;

    entity PurchaseOrderHeader as projection on PO.HeaderView {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PurchaseOrderId
    };
}

The converted code includes comments linking back to the original source files - making it easy for users to verify that key business logic has been accurately transformed.  In the similar way, all xsjslib and xsjs files are re-written as SAP CAP custom handlers. The resulting  modularised SAP CAP compliant project mirrors the original directory structure, ensuring familiarity and easy traceability.  All SAP HANA objects  will be adjusted automatically to follow SAP HANA Cloud naming conventions.

Additionally, the assistant generates a detailed report outlining recommended post-migration steps to guide users through final adjustments

As generative AI conversion is not guaranteed to be 100% accurate, human intervention is required post-migration to validate, refine, and productionize the application logic and service definitions.  

References

  • For prerequisites and step by step guide refer to corresponding xsc-to-cap  and xsa-to-cap repositories. 
  • Help Document: Migrating XS Classic Applications to SAP CAP
  • Help Document: SAP HANA XS Advanced Migration Guide
